Gollala Mamidada Kodanda Ramalayam

# Gollala Mamidada Kodanda Ramalayam

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Mar 22, 2026

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

3

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!yeiW!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fddcf309a-6f39-4db5-bf29-fdec2c750216_3420x2538.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!yeiW!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fddcf309a-6f39-4db5-bf29-fdec2c750216_3420x2538.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The **Kodandarama Temple** at **Gollala Mamidada** in East Godavari district stands as one of the most revered shrines dedicated to Lord Rama in Andhra Pradesh. Nestled on the tranquil banks of the **Tulyabhaga River**, the temple is celebrated for its **majestic gopurams**, which soar skyward with striking elegance and intricate craftsmanship. Their towering presence dominates the landscape, drawing pilgrims and visitors from afar.

Often referred to as **“Chinna Bhadradri”**, the temple carries a symbolic connection to the famed Bhadrachalam Temple, embodying a similar aura of devotion and grandeur. The sanctum radiates serenity, while the architectural splendor of the gopurams reflects both artistic mastery and spiritual depth. The riverside setting enhances its sacred atmosphere, making the temple not only a center of worship but also a cultural landmark that preserves the devotional heritage of the region.

This blend of natural beauty, architectural magnificence, and spiritual resonance has made Kodandarama Temple a cherished destination, where faith and artistry converge in timeless harmony.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!c6Hz!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe258f91b-2ab2-4d57-bcfd-a7cab87d906f_275x183.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!c6Hz!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe258f91b-2ab2-4d57-bcfd-a7cab87d906f_275x183.png)

was first established in **1889** by two brothers, **Dwarampudi Subbi Reddy and Rami Reddy**, who generously donated land and constructed a modest shrine with **wooden idols of Rama and Sita**. This humble beginning reflected their deep faith and commitment to the community.

As devotion grew, so did the temple. In **1939**, a larger and more elaborate structure was built, elevating the sanctity and architectural presence of the site. The temple’s iconic identity was further shaped by the construction of its two towering **gopurams**—one completed in **1948** and the other in **1956**. These monumental gateways, rising high above the surrounding landscape, transformed the temple into a landmark of both spiritual and artistic significance.

Its two monumental **gopurams** dominate the skyline: the **east-facing tower**, rising to about **160–170 feet**, is built with **nine storeys** and crowned by **five kalasams**, while the **west-facing gopuram** is even more imposing, reaching nearly **200–210 feet**, with **eleven storeys** and the same five kalasams.

Adding to its uniqueness, in **1975** a **mirror hall** was constructed between the two mandapams, positioned just above the **garbhagriha**. This hall is adorned with a striking **stucco relief of Sri Rama Pattabhishekam**, capturing the divine coronation scene, while on the opposite side, another relief depicts **Lord Rama blessing Hanuman**. The mirror hall, shimmering with reflections, creates a sense of celestial radiance, amplifying the temple’s spiritual atmosphere.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WmKn!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd731ba2e-3e0a-48f0-b8a6-5e5ff7481545_296x168.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WmKn!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd731ba2e-3e0a-48f0-b8a6-5e5ff7481545_296x168.png)

Its two towering gopurams are not only monumental in scale but also richly adorned with **sculptures depicting episodes from the Ramayana, Mahabharata, and Bhagavatam**, spread across all four sides. These carvings transform the gateways into living canvases of epic storytelling, allowing devotees to visually journey through the great narratives of dharma and devotion as they enter the temple.

The temple’s **sikhara** adds another dimension of sacred art, featuring intricate sculptures from the **Bala Ramayanam**, capturing the childhood episodes of Lord Rama with tenderness and detail. This emphasis on narrative sculpture makes the temple a cultural archive, where stone and stucco preserve the timeless stories of India’s spiritual heritage.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!UFmr!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F56a67dc9-fce7-4dab-9ffc-cdbd5688be5d_800x449.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!UFmr!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F56a67dc9-fce7-4dab-9ffc-cdbd5688be5d_800x449.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!qDcO!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F79796856-1bbd-418d-81a4-e599528e72aa_819x1024.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!qDcO!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F79796856-1bbd-418d-81a4-e599528e72aa_819x1024.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

At its heart, the **garbhalayam** enshrines the divine presence of **Lord Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana**, with **Hanuman** standing in eternal service. This sanctum radiates serenity, anchoring the temple’s spiritual aura.

Pilgrims can ascend the 2 towering gopurams through nearly **300 steps**, reaching the summit for a breathtaking view of the surrounding countryside, where the lush East Godavari landscape stretches endlessly.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!i6ZT!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe77b44de-4633-4dbd-baaa-69178a023851_296x168.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!i6ZT!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe77b44de-4633-4dbd-baaa-69178a023851_296x168.png)

A sacred **pushkarni**, fed by the waters of the **Tulyabhaga River**, adds to the temple’s sanctity, offering a place of ritual purity and reflection.

Sri Rama Navami is grandly celebrated in the temple, that features the Sita Rama Kalyanam. The District collector along with the local MLA, offer the pattu vastrams(silk clothes) on this occasion. Vaikunta Ekadasi and Vijayadasami too are grandly celebrated here.

Alongside the famed Kodandarama Temple, **Gollala Mamidada** also houses the **Suryanarayana Temple**, dedicated to the Sun God. Though not as prominent as the celebrated **Arasavalli Surya Temple** in Srikakulam, this shrine is equally worth visiting for its serene atmosphere and architectural charm.

The temple’s design closely mirrors that of the **Kodandarama Temple**, with similar gopuram style and layout, reflecting the shared cultural and artistic traditions of the region. Nestled amid the same lush surroundings of **paddy fields and coconut groves**, the Suryanarayana Temple offers a tranquil spiritual experience, complementing the grandeur of its Rama counterpart.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!8cqj!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffca2c317-f752-462c-b6c4-e0599c2ee3dd_680x510.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!8cqj!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffca2c317-f752-462c-b6c4-e0599c2ee3dd_680x510.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Built in **1920** by **Kovvuri Basivi Reddy Garu**, the zamindar of the village renowned for his philanthropy and charitable works, the temple reflects both generosity and spiritual dedication. He donated nearly **16 acres of fertile wetland** for its upkeep, ensuring that the shrine would flourish as a center of worship.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!jIIw!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe8e0ef86-83a5-46c7-a422-d4db803ce7df_559x447.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!jIIw!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe8e0ef86-83a5-46c7-a422-d4db803ce7df_559x447.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

With its twin temples and lush surroundings, **Gollala Mamidada**—affectionately called **Gopurala Mamidada**—has become a cherished pilgrimage destination in the **Godavari districts**. The towering gopurams of the **Kodandarama Temple**, the serene sanctity of the **Suryanarayana Temple**, and the tranquil **pushkarni** fed by the **Tulyabhaga River** together create a sacred landscape that blends devotion with natural beauty.

The village is often hailed as **“Konaseema Ayodhya”** and **“Chinna Bhadradri”**, titles that reflect both its spiritual stature and its architectural grandeur. Surrounded by **paddy fields and coconut groves**, the temples stand as radiant beacons of faith, artistry, and philanthropy.

Gollala Mamidada is well connected by road from **Kakinada, Rajahmundry, and Vizag**, making it accessible to pilgrims across the region. While there are **no guest houses or lodging facilities**,in keeping with the tradition that worshippers offer prayers and depart immediately, the temple administration ensures that **pilgrims have good amenities** for their visit.

In essence, Gollala Mamidada is not just a village of temples; it is a **living spiritual tapestry**, where devotion, heritage, and nature converge, offering visitors a glimpse of timeless faith rooted in the heart of Andhra Pradesh.

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
